items.prompt: In a classic Verilog TB, inputs driven in initial are usually…
items.explain: Procedural assignment in initial requires reg/logic; wire is for continuous or DUT drive.

items.prompt: A DUT output observed by the TB is typically…
choices: A wire or logic net the TB reads but does not procedural-assign
A reg the TB drives every cycle
A parameter copied from the DUT
items.explain: The TB samples DUT outputs; driving them from initial is a mistake.

items.prompt: The DUT instance line in the TB mainly…
choices: Connects TB nets to DUT ports
items.explain: Port connections wire stimulus and observe nets between TB and design.

items.prompt: Procedural assignment to a wire in initial is a common beginner mistake.
items.explain: The wire-stimulus preset in the lab shows this compile/runtime trap.

choices: Forces all outputs to zero permanently
Replaces the need for a clock
items.explain: finish stops simulation after checks and displays complete.
